meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Escape Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop spinning your wheels in tutorial hell. Learn how to implement production-grade analytics to validate your SaaS MVP using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Escape tutorial hell by shifting from passive consumption to data-driven shipping. Implement a lightweight event-tracking schema that maps directly to your KPIs. By automating your analytics integration with Claude Code and Antigravity, you transform abstract code into actionable user behavior insights, proving your market fit faster than your competitors.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks watching tutorials, tweaking boilerplate code, and chasing the phantom of the 'perfect' architecture. Yet, your dashboard remains empty, and your users are ghosts. This isn't a lack of effort; it's the paralysis of tutorial hell, where you build for the sake of building rather than for the sake of business validation.

True founders don't just write code; they instrument reality. By integrating professional-grade analytics into your MVP, you force yourself to confront the brutal truth of user engagement. It is time to stop guessing and start measuring. This guide provides the tactical blueprint to automate your telemetry, allowing you to stop building in the dark and start scaling based on real-world data.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the single event that represents value for your user. This prevents 'event bloat' and keeps your data clean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my SaaS business model for a productivity app and suggest 5 high-signal events that define user retention and activation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a schema.json file based on the top 5 high-signal events identified for user retention tracking.'

steps.title: Automate Telemetry Injection
steps.description: Use AI to inject tracking hooks into your core user flows without manually editing every component file.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable React hook 'useAnalytics' that wraps trackEvent calls for PostHog or Mixpanel, ensuring consistent event naming conventions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'Inject the useAnalytics hook into the AuthProvider and Dashboard components to track login and project creation events.'

steps.title: Implement Server-Side Validation
steps.description: Client-side tracking is unreliable. Ensure your backend broadcasts critical events to your analytics provider to capture user drop-offs accurately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a node.js middleware that intercepts critical database mutations and forwards them as events to our analytics API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the backend user controller to include the analytics middleware for sign-up and subscription events.'

steps.title: Build Automated Dashboards
steps.description: Visualize your data immediately. If you can't see the conversion funnel in 5 minutes, your analytics setup is too complex.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a PostHog dashboard configuration JSON that visualizes user activation, churn, and feature usage based on our event schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Upload the dashboard configuration to the analytics provider API and verify connectivity.'

steps.title: Create Feedback Loops
steps.description: Configure automated alerts for when your core metric drops, turning your analytics from a static report into an active notification system.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script that monitors daily event counts and sends a Slack alert if user sign-ups drop below the 7-day moving average.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'Integrate the daily-alert script into our CI/CD pipeline as a scheduled cron job.'

pro_tips: Never track everything. If an event doesn't inform a product decision, delete it from your schema to reduce noise.
Use 'Property Enrichment' to attach user roles (e.g., 'free_tier', 'enterprise') to every event for segmented analysis.
Always test events in a staging environment before pushing to production to avoid polluting your historical data.
Implement a 'catch-all' error event that logs client-side JS crashes directly to your analytics tool for faster debugging.
Treat your analytics code as a first-class citizen; keep it decoupled from your UI logic using an abstraction layer.
resources: PostHog Event Schema Best Practices: https://posthog.com/docs
content.next_steps: Now that your telemetry is live, spend the next 48 hours observing real user behavior in your staging environment. Once you see the first event log, pivot your development focus exclusively to the feature that drives the highest conversion rate identified by your data.
content.conversion_block: Stop building features that nobody uses and start shipping products that solve real problems. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
